input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Install Xcode (for iOS)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- After install, run:
  ```bash
  xcode-select --install          # command-line tools
  sudo xcodebuild -license accept # accept license
  ```
- Open Xcode → **Settings → Platforms** → install the iOS Simulator runtime.

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest **LTS or STS** that supports MAUI.
- Discover the right version: on the download page, the MAUI workload version matrix is listed under "Workloads." Alternatively:
  ```bash
  dotnet workload search maui   # shows compatible MAUI workload versions
  ```
- Verify:
  ```bash
  dotnet --version
  ```

## 3. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```
- Verify:
  ```bash
  dotnet workload list           # should show "maui"
  ```

## 4. Install Java JDK (for Android)

- The MAUI workload installs Microsoft's OpenJDK automatically. Confirm with:
  ```bash
  dotnet workload list           # "android" workload should appear
  ```
- If you need to install manually: **Microsoft Build of OpenJDK** from [learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download](https://learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download) (JDK 17).

## 5. Android SDK & Emulator

- The `maui` workload pulls in the `android` workload, which auto-provisions the Android SDK on first build. To manage it explicitly:
  ```bash
  dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies \
    -f net9.0-android \
    "-p:AndroidSdkDirectory=$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" \
    -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true
  ```
- For the emulator, install **Android Studio** from [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio), then use **SDK Manager** to install emulator images — or use the command-line `sdkmanager`.

## 6. (Optional) IDE

| IDE | Source |
|---|---|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit + .NET MAUI extension | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

> Visual Studio for Mac has been retired — VS Code or Rider are the recommended options.

## 7. Verify Everything End-to-End

```bash
# Create a new project
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios

# Run on iOS simulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios -t:Run \
  -p:_DeviceName=:v2:udid=<SIMULATOR_UDID>

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run
```

Replace `net9.0` with your actual target framework (check `dotnet --list-sdks`).

## 8. Troubleshooting Checklist

| Symptom | Fix |
|---|---|
| `xcode-select: error` | `xcode-select --install` |
| Android licenses not accepted | Add `-p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true` |
| MAUI workload missing | `sudo dotnet workload restore` in project dir |
| Simulator not found | Open Xcode → Settings → Platforms → download runtime |

Let me provide a comprehensive but concise plan.
